meta.description: Generate platform-specific thumbnails from representative video frames with brand overlay

step.name: identify_hook_scenes
step.action: Analyze the SSD to identify hook scenes (scenes tagged as hook, intro, or high-impact). These scenes typically contain the most visually compelling content for thumbnails. If no scenes are explicitly tagged, select the first scene and any scenes marked with high visual_impact scores. Identify 2 to 3 candidate scenes for thumbnail extraction.
step.output: List of 2 to 3 candidate hook scenes for thumbnail extraction

step.name: extract_representative_frame
step.action: For each candidate scene, extract a representative frame using FFmpeg. Select the frame at the visual peak of the scene (typically 30 to 50 percent through the scene duration). Use ffmpeg -ss {timestamp} -i {video} -frames:v 1 -q:v 2 {output}.png to extract a high-quality PNG frame. Evaluate each extracted frame for visual clarity, composition, and absence of motion blur. Select the best frame as the primary thumbnail base.
step.output: Extracted representative frame (PNG) for thumbnail base

step.name: apply_brand_overlay
step.action: Apply brand overlay elements to the selected frame. Add the episode title text with brand font, size, and color. Apply a subtle gradient or darkened region behind the title text for readability. Optionally add the channel logo or watermark in a consistent position. Apply any brand-specific visual treatment (color grading, vignette, border). Ensure the overlay enhances visual appeal without obscuring the key visual content.

step.name: resize_per_platform
step.action: Resize the branded thumbnail for each target platform. YouTube: 1280x720 pixels (16:9). Instagram Post: 1080x1080 pixels (1:1, center-crop or recompose). TikTok: 1080x1920 pixels (9:16, center-crop or recompose). Use high-quality resampling (lanczos). For aspect ratio changes, use smart cropping to preserve the key visual area and title text. Adjust title text position if needed for non-native aspect ratios.
step.output: Platform-specific thumbnail files at correct dimensions

step.name: save_thumbnails
step.action: Save all generated thumbnails to episodes/{episode_id}/exports/thumbnails/. Use the naming convention: {episode_id}-thumb-{platform}-{timestamp}.png. Save in PNG format for maximum quality. Also generate JPEG versions at 85% quality for platforms with file size limits. Create a thumbnail manifest listing all generated files with their dimensions, file sizes, and target platforms.

quality-gates.gate: 2 to 3 hook scenes identified from the SSD
Representative frames extracted at visual peaks without motion blur
Brand overlay applied with title text, logo, and visual treatment
Title text readable against background (gradient or darkened region)
Smart cropping preserves key visual content across aspect ratios
Thumbnails saved in PNG and JPEG formats with manifest
